QF.id: Role of RTAs in Mutual Fund Transactions_36

question.text: Which of the following functions does an RTA perform in relation to the IEPF (Investor Education and Protection Fund)?

answer.text: Transferring unclaimed dividends and corresponding units to IEPF after 7 years
Managing the IEPF corpus and investing it in government securities
Distributing IEPF funds to eligible mutual fund investors annually
Auditing the IEPF accounts on behalf of SEBI

explanation.text: **The Logic:** When dividends or redemption proceeds remain unclaimed for 7 years, the RTA facilitates their transfer to the IEPF managed by the Ministry of Corporate Affairs (MCA). The RTA identifies and processes these transfers. **The Trap:** The RTA doesn't manage IEPF funds -- that's MCA's role. The RTA's function is limited to identifying unclaimed amounts and transferring them to IEPF after the 7-year period. **Cross-Exam Context:** IEPF provisions appear in NISM Series II-B and also in Series V-A. The 7-year timeline is frequently tested. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Track clients whose dividends might be unclaimed. A proactive call to update bank details or claim pending dividends before the 7-year IEPF transfer deadline is excellent client service and prevents permanent loss of access.
